title: feat: agentmap next command — single-file prompt for small models
description:
Small models fail when given the full index-tasks.md (84+ files, 1000+ lines). They lose context before reaching the actual work, get confused by the project's own AGENTS.md, and try to process everything at once.

agentmap next reads the task list, finds the first unchecked entry, reads the current nav block from that file, and prints a tight self-contained prompt for exactly one file. The agent rewrites the nav block, runs agentmap update <file> (which auto-checks the entry), then calls agentmap next again for the next file.

Design:
- No args: auto-find task list by searching upward for .agentmap/index-tasks.md from cwd
- Optional path arg: agentmap next <path/to/index-tasks.md>
- Output: file path + current AGENT:NAV block + rewrite instructions + two commands to run
- --count N: emit N consecutive prompts (separated) in one call
- Done state: print completion message + agentmap check command
- Exit 0 always (caller reads stdout)
close_reason: feat(next) shipped in b00ab41; agentmap next and --count N working on covenant (84-file project)

title: Parser misses headings when code fence contains blank lines or inline backticks
description: The fence-toggle in internal/parser/parser.go fires on any line whose trimmed content starts with triple-backtick. If a code block contains a blank line, or if any non-fence line between the closing fence and the next heading starts with triple-backtick, the parser prematurely re-enters fence mode. The orphaned fence then swallows all headings until the next closing triple-backtick in the file. Observed: in testdata/design-clean.md, sections 4.3 through 8.1 (lines ~274–521) are entirely invisible to the parser because of a spurious fence re-entry after the section 4.2 code block closes at ~line 257. The generated nav block is missing 6 sections as a result.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Parser correctly extracts all headings from testdata/design-clean.md including 4.3 through 8.1. 2. Regression test added using a minimal fixture with a fenced code block containing blank lines and/or inline backtick lines. 3. make ci passes.
close_reason: Fixed: anchor <!-- detection to start-of-line; regression tests added; CI passes.

title: Add ~ prefix marker for auto-generated keyword descriptions
description: Add ~ prefix to all keyword-generated about and purpose fields in nav blocks to distinguish auto-generated content from agent/human-written descriptions. Requires changes to generate (prefix output); navblock (IsAutoGenerated helper; preserve ~ in parsing); update (preserve ~ prefix); check (optionally warn about remaining ~ descriptions); and design doc (document the convention). This is the foundation that index and other commands build on to detect unreviewed descriptions.
design:
## The ~ prefix convention

All keyword-extracted descriptions (purpose and about fields) produced by generate are prefixed with ~. This signals: auto-generated; never reviewed by an agent or human.

Example nav block with ~ markers:
  purpose:~token OAuth2 authentication flow
  sections[3]{heading;lines;about}:
  ## Token Lifecycle;45-112;~token refresh expiry rotation
  ## OAuth2 Exchange;113-198;~code exchange redirect >hint1;hint2;hint3
  ### Token Storage;55-80;~storage encryption keychain

When an agent rewrites a description it removes the ~:
  purpose:token lifecycle; OAuth2 exchange flow

### Code changes

1. internal/generate/generate.go — buildNavEntries and purpose extraction
   - Prefix all keyword-derived About fields with ~
   - Prefix purpose line with ~
   - Only when keywords are auto-generated (not when preserving existing descriptions)

2. internal/navblock/navblock.go — helper function
   - IsAutoGenerated(s string) bool — returns strings.HasPrefix(s; "~")
   - TrimAutoGenerated(s string) string — returns strings.TrimPrefix(s; "~")
   - No changes to parsing — ~ is just part of the string value

3. internal/update/update.go — no changes needed
   - update preserves existing about fields as-is (already correct behavior)
   - ~ prefix survives update because update never rewrites descriptions

4. internal/check/check.go — optional enhancement
   - Add a warning category for files with remaining ~ descriptions
   - Not blocking; just informational (could be a --warn-unreviewed flag)

5. agentmap-design.md — document the convention
   - Add to section 5 (Description Tiers) explaining ~ as the Tier 1 marker
   - Add to section 4.1 (generate command) noting that output uses ~

### Testing

- generate_test.go: verify ~ prefix on all keyword-generated about and purpose fields
- generate_test.go: verify ~ is NOT added when descriptions come from existing nav blocks (update path)
- navblock_test.go: test IsAutoGenerated and TrimAutoGenerated helpers
- Integration test: generate a file; verify ~ in output; manually edit to remove ~; re-run generate with --force; verify ~ returns

### Migration

Existing nav blocks without ~ are treated as agent-written (no ~ = already reviewed). This is the correct default — if someone already has nav blocks; they wrote the descriptions themselves or accepted the keywords.
close_reason: Added ~ prefix marker to all keyword-generated purpose and about fields in nav blocks. Added IsAutoGenerated/TrimAutoGenerated helpers to navblock package. Updated design doc with ~ convention. 3 new tests for ~ prefix behavior.

title: Design: bulk index command for doc-library RAG use case
description: New use case: a large markdown library (hundreds of files in organized folders) used as an explore-agent RAG. Needs a command (agentmap index?) that recursively generates nav blocks with full high-quality descriptions in one pass. Key differences from current generate: scale (hundreds of files); description quality is critical (Tier 1 keywords insufficient; need agent/LLM descriptions); one-shot onboarding not incremental; may lack git context. Consider: dedicated index command vs enhanced generate; batching and resume-on-failure; integration as slash command or agent skill for cross-tool compatibility; how explore-agents consume the nav blocks for retrieval.
design:
## Command: agentmap index <path>

Two-phase approach: automated skeleton generation followed by agent-driven description writing.

### Phase 1: CLI generates skeletons + task list

1. **Discover** — discovery.DiscoverFiles(path; cfg.Exclude); also excludes .agentmap/
2. **Classify** each file:
   - No nav block → generate skeleton; add to task list
   - Nav block with any ~ descriptions → skip generate; add to task list
   - Nav block with no ~ descriptions → skip entirely (already indexed)
3. **Generate** skeletons — reuse generate.File; all keyword-derived about and purpose fields get ~ prefix
4. **Build task list** — for each unfinished file emit:
   - File path; line count; section count
   - Purpose checkbox with current ~ text
   - Sections checkbox with count + listed entries (including > compressed hints)
   - See entries checkbox
5. **Write** .agentmap/index-tasks.md — header references canonical instruction doc + progress counter + file entries
6. **Print** summary to stdout pointing agent to the task file

### Phase 2: Agent works through the task list

Agent reads .agentmap/index-tasks.md; follows canonical instructions (separate doc) to:
- Read each file's sections using line ranges from the task list
- Rewrite ~ prefixed descriptions with clear human-quality descriptions
- Add see entries for related files
- Mark checkboxes as complete

### The ~ marker for auto-generated descriptions

Prefix on all auto-generated keyword descriptions: about:~token refresh expiry
- Applied in generate when building nav entries (both purpose and about fields)
- Detected via strings.HasPrefix(s; "~")
- Agent removes ~ when rewriting: about:token lifecycle; OAuth2 exchange flow
- check command can optionally warn about remaining ~ descriptions
- update command preserves ~ prefix (it never writes descriptions)

### Task list format

File: .agentmap/index-tasks.md



Three nav entry types in the task list:
- Normal section: ## Heading [Ls-e] (~keywords)
- Compressed subsections: ## Heading [Ls-e] (~keywords >hint1;hint2;hint3)
- Expanded h3s: each h3 listed separately with own (~keywords)

### Flags

- --dry-run — show what would happen without writing files
- --force — regenerate skeletons even for files with existing nav blocks

### Implementation changes

| Change | Package | Scope |
|--------|---------|-------|
| Add ~ prefix to keyword-generated about/purpose | internal/generate/ | Small |
| IsAutoGenerated() helper | internal/navblock/ | Small |
| New index package: orchestration; classification; task list writer | internal/index/ | Medium |
| New indexCmd cobra command | cmd/agentmap/ | Small |
| .agentmap/ in default exclude list | internal/config/ | Trivial |
| Create .agentmap/ directory if needed | internal/index/ | Trivial |

### Dependencies

- Canonical agent instructions doc (separate bead) — referenced from task file header
- Project-level file index (agentmap-uzh) — downstream; uses per-file nav blocks produced by index
close_reason: Implemented: agentmap index command with task list generation and adaptive files block; merged to main in 7df19f7

title: Design: switch nav format from s,e to s,n (start + length)
description: The Read tool takes offset and limit (length); not offset and end. Current s,e format requires agents to compute e-s+1 to get the limit arg. Switching to s,n (start; line-count) would map directly to Read(offset=s; limit=n) with zero arithmetic. Trade-off: n is less human-readable than e when debugging; but generate -D exists for that. This touches every package and ~45 test fixtures so it should be decided before more code is built on the current format.
design:
## Options

### Option A: s,e (current)
```
36,50,##Token Refresh,silent rotation and expiry
```
Agent: `Read(offset=36, limit=50-36+1)` — subtraction required.

### Option B: s,n (start + length) — RECOMMENDED
```
nav[N]{s,n,name,about}:
36,15,##Token Refresh,silent rotation and expiry
```
Agent: `Read(offset=36, limit=15)` — direct passthrough; zero math.
Header changes from `{s,e,...}` to `{s,n,...}` which is self-documenting.

### Option C: s only (infer end from next entry)
```
36,##Token Refresh,silent rotation and expiry
51,##Token Revocation,logout and forced invalidation
```
Saves one field per line but: last section needs EOF sentinel; nested h3-inside-h2 breaks ordering assumption; agent must reason about hierarchy. Wrong tradeoff — pushes complexity into expensive agent reasoning.

## Migration scope
- internal/navblock: change NavEntry struct (End -> Len or NumLines); update parser and writer
- internal/parser/sections: ComputeSections returns length instead of end (or add helper)
- internal/generate: adjust offset math
- internal/update: shift reporting
- internal/check: validation comparison
- internal/gitutil: LineRange may stay as start/end internally; only the nav format changes
- All test fixtures (~45 occurrences of nav[N]{s,e,...})
- AGENTS.md; README.md; agentmap-design.md: change Read instructions
- Agent skill templates: change Read instructions

## Recommendation
Option B. Do it now before more code and test fixtures accumulate.
close_reason: Switched nav block format from s,e to s,n. NavEntry.End renamed to N (line count). Header changed to {s,n,name,about}. Added Section.Len() helper. All tests and docs updated.

title: fix: section computation duplicates and missing headings
description:

1. Duplicate h3 entries (###3.1-3.5 appear twice in nav block)

Root cause likely: section end-line computation not respecting heading depth boundaries. An h3 section is probably extending until EOF or next h1 instead of stopping at the next h2.

Design doc has: # (h1) -> ##3. Format Specification (h2) -> ###3.1-3.5 (h3) -> ##4. CLI Commands (h2) -> ... -> ##13. Future Work (h2) -> ###13.2-13.6 (h3)

Expected: h3 children should be contained within their h2 parent. Actual: h3 sections appear to swallow everything until next h3 or EOF.

Write failing tests first, then fix ComputeSections.

title: Canonical agent instructions for filling out AGENT:NAV blocks
description: Create a markdown document with canonical instructions that agents follow when writing AGENT:NAV block descriptions. Referenced by the index task list and potentially by agent skills/rules. Covers: how to read a section and write a good about field (under 10 words; no commas; focus on agent decision-making); how to write purpose lines; how to identify and add see entries; how to handle compressed >hint subsections; the ~ prefix convention for auto-generated keywords; quality criteria for descriptions.
design:
## Canonical Agent Instructions for AGENT:NAV Description Writing

### Document location

docs/nav-writing-guide.md (or similar — co-located with agentmap docs)

### Purpose

A single reference document that:
1. The index task list header links to
2. Agent skill templates can embed or reference
3. Humans can read to understand the conventions

### Content outline

#### 1. Reading a file before writing descriptions
- Read the AGENT:NAV block first (first 20 lines)
- Use line ranges from the task list: Read(offset=s; limit=n)
- Skim the full section content before writing

#### 2. Writing a purpose line
- One line summarizing the entire file
- Under 10 words
- No commas (use semicolons for lists)
- Focus: what would an agent need to know to decide whether to open this file?
- Remove the ~ prefix when rewriting

#### 3. Writing about fields for sections
- One line per section; under 10 words
- No commas
- Focus: what would an agent need to know to decide whether to read this section?
- For compressed sections (>hint1;hint2;hint3): read the subsections and write an about that covers the parent section as a whole
- Remove the ~ prefix when rewriting

#### 4. Adding see entries
- List files that are closely related (cross-referenced; co-dependent; complementary)
- Format: see:other-file.md;another-file.md
- Look for: markdown links to other files; shared concepts; files that an agent reading this file would likely need next
- Do not add see entries for every file — only genuinely useful navigation links

#### 5. The ~ convention
- ~ prefix means auto-generated keywords; never reviewed by an agent
- Removing ~ signals that a human or agent has reviewed and improved the description
- The index task list only includes files/sections that still have ~ descriptions

#### 6. Quality criteria
- Would an agent reading only this description know whether to read the section? (decision support)
- Is it specific enough to distinguish from sibling sections? (disambiguation)
- Does it avoid generic words like 'overview' or 'introduction' unless truly accurate? (precision)
- Under 10 words? No commas? (format compliance)

### Relationship to other docs
- agentmap-design.md section 5 defines the tiered description system
- This doc is the practical guide for Tier 2 (agent-written) descriptions
- Skill templates (agentmap-3vh) should reference or embed a condensed version
close_reason: Created docs/nav-writing-guide.md: canonical agent reference for writing AGENT:NAV descriptions. Covers all 6 required topics with good/bad examples. Reviewed and fixed 2 critical findings. Merged to main.

title: Project-level file index (files block)
description: Generate a project-level index that aggregates all per-file purpose lines into a single nav block. Format: files[N]{path;about}: with directory prefix lines (no comma) grouping file entries (have comma). Directory prefixes reduce token cost by not repeating paths. Flat; deterministic; no LLM needed — just reads existing nav block purpose fields. Placed in AGENTS.md or a dedicated index file. Solves cold-start problem: agent scans ~2000 tokens to find the right file in a 200-file library.
design:
## Format

```
<!-- AGENT:NAV
purpose:project file index for [repo-name]
files[N]{path,about}:
README.md,project overview and quickstart
CONTRIBUTING.md,contribution guidelines
docs/
authentication.md,token lifecycle; OAuth2 exchange
error-policy.md,retry logic; circuit breakers
docs/api/
endpoints.md,REST endpoint catalog
rate-limiting.md,rate limit tiers; burst allowance
webhooks.md,webhook registration; payload signing
docs/guides/
quickstart.md,first API call; auth setup
migration-v2.md,breaking changes from v1; migration steps
-->
```

## Parsing Rule

- Line with no comma = directory prefix (applies to subsequent entries)
- Line with comma = file entry (full path = last prefix + filename)
- Root-level files appear before any prefix
- No new syntax needed — falls out of existing CSV convention

## Generation

1. Walk all markdown files that have AGENT:NAV blocks
2. Extract each file's `purpose:` field
3. Group by directory; sort directories alphabetically; files alphabetically within
4. Emit directory prefix lines + file entries
5. `N` = total file entries (not counting directory prefix lines)

## Placement: Adaptive by Size

Threshold: configurable via `index_inline_max` in agentmap.yml (default: ~20 files).

**Small projects (under threshold):**
- Inline the files block directly in AGENTS.md inside the agentmap:init section
- No extra files; everything in one place

**Larger projects (over threshold):**
- Write dedicated AGENTMAP.md at repo root
- Add a one-line pointer in AGENTS.md: `See AGENTMAP.md for the full file index.`
- Keeps AGENTS.md lean; agents follow the pointer

The `init` and `index` commands handle this automatically. If a project grows past the threshold; re-running index migrates from inline to dedicated file (and vice versa if files are removed).

## Token Budget

~8-12 tokens per file entry. Directory prefixes save ~5-8 tokens per grouped file.
200 files across 20 dirs: ~2000 tokens total (vs ~400k to read all files).
20-file inline index in AGENTS.md: ~200 tokens — negligible.
Pays for itself on first use.

## Open Questions

- Should files without nav blocks still appear (with filename only; no about)?
- Should the index include non-markdown files that agents commonly need (config files etc)?
- Update semantics: regenerate entirely on each run? Or preserve manual edits?

title: 7.8 agentmap uninit + uninstall
description: uninit: reverse init by finding <!-- agentmap:init --> markers; removing appended content; deleting created files; removing hook entries. uninstall: detect install method (Homebrew/Scoop/go/direct) and defer or remove binary. --yes/--dry-run/--keep-config flags. Never touch nav blocks; only init-injected content.
design:
## uninit: Reverse init

### Block Removal Strategy

Templates use paired markers:
```
<!-- agentmap:init -->
... content ...
<!-- /agentmap:init -->
```

For files where content was appended (AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md; .cursorrules):
1. Read the file
2. Find `<!-- agentmap:init -->` start marker
3. Find `<!-- /agentmap:init -->` end marker  
4. Remove everything from start marker through end marker (inclusive)
5. Trim any resulting double-blank-lines at the removal point
6. If file is now empty (only whitespace): delete it ONLY if no content existed before the agentmap block (heuristic: if marker was at line 1 or preceded only by blank lines)

For files init created outright (.cursor/rules/agentmap.md; .windsurf/rules/agentmap.md; etc.):
1. Check that the file contains the `<!-- agentmap:init -->` marker (safety: don't delete files we didn't create)
2. Delete the file
3. If the parent directory is now empty AND was created by init (e.g. `.opencode/skills/agentmap/`): remove empty dirs up to the tool config root

### Hook Removal

For .pre-commit-config.yaml:
- Parse the YAML; find and remove the entry with `id: agentmap-check`
- If the containing `repo: local` block has no other hooks; remove the entire repo block
- Write back

For .git/hooks/pre-commit and .husky/pre-commit:
- Find the agentmap guard block (from `# agentmap: validate` comment to the closing `fi`)
- Remove it
- If the file is now empty (only shebang + whitespace): delete it

### Output

```
Will remove from: AGENTS.md (28 lines; agentmap instructions block)
Will delete: .cursor/rules/agentmap.md
Will remove from: .pre-commit-config.yaml (agentmap-check hook entry)

Proceed? [y/N]
```

If no markers found anywhere: print `agentmap not initialized in this project` and exit 0.

### Flags
- `--yes`: skip confirmation
- `--dry-run`: preview only

## uninstall: Remove the binary

### Install Method Detection

```go
exe, _ := os.Executable()
exePath, _ := filepath.EvalSymlinks(exe)

switch {
case strings.Contains(exePath, "/Cellar/") || strings.Contains(exePath, "/homebrew/"):
    fmt.Println("Installed via Homebrew. Run: brew uninstall agentmap")
    return nil
case strings.Contains(exePath, "\\scoop\\"):
    fmt.Println("Installed via Scoop. Run: scoop uninstall agentmap")
    return nil
case isUnderGoPath(exePath):
    fmt.Println("Installed via go install. Run: go clean -i github.com/ryankelln/agentmap/cmd/agentmap")
    return nil
default:
    // Direct install -- remove the binary
}
```

`isUnderGoPath` checks if the binary is under `$GOPATH/bin` or `$GOBIN`.

For managed installs (Homebrew/Scoop/go): NEVER force-remove. Print the correct uninstall command and exit. The package manager owns the binary.

For direct installs: remove the binary. If in a protected dir (/usr/local/bin); may need sudo.

### Flags
- `--yes`: skip confirmation
- `--keep-config`: only remove binary; don't run uninit

### Flow
1. If `--keep-config` not set: check if current directory (or git root) has agentmap config; if so; run uninit logic first
2. Detect install method
3. If managed: print instructions and exit
4. If direct: confirm; then `os.Remove(exePath)`

### Safety
- uninit never touches AGENT:NAV blocks inside markdown content -- only the init-injected instruction blocks
- uninit never deletes files that don't contain the agentmap:init marker
- uninstall defers to package managers; never force-removes a managed binary
close_reason: uninit removes init blocks and hook entries; uninstall detects method and defers to package managers; CI passes

title: 7.5 Embedded agent skill templates
description: Go embed templates per tool format: Cursor (globs frontmatter); Windsurf (trigger frontmatter); Continue (name/globs frontmatter); Roo Code; Amazon Q (plain markdown); OpenCode (.opencode/skills/agentmap/SKILL.md). Written by init.
design:
## Embedded Skill Templates

Use Go `embed` to ship templates inside the binary. Create `internal/templates/` directory.

### Directory Structure

```
internal/templates/
  agents.md.tmpl        # For AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md (plain markdown append block)
  cursor.md.tmpl        # .cursor/rules/agentmap.md (with Cursor frontmatter)
  windsurf.md.tmpl      # .windsurf/rules/agentmap.md (with Windsurf frontmatter)
  continue.md.tmpl      # .continue/rules/agentmap.md (with Continue frontmatter)
  roo.md.tmpl           # .roo/rules/agentmap.md (plain markdown)
  amazonq.md.tmpl       # .amazonq/rules/agentmap.md (plain markdown)
  opencode-skill.md.tmpl # .opencode/skills/agentmap/SKILL.md (with OpenCode frontmatter)
  hook-git.sh.tmpl      # Git hook script snippet
  hook-precommit.yml.tmpl # pre-commit-config.yaml entry
  embed.go              # //go:embed directives
```

### Template Content

Every template MUST start with the idempotency marker. For append templates (agents.md.tmpl):
```markdown
<!-- agentmap:init -->
## Reading Markdown Files

Every markdown file over 50 lines has an AGENT:NAV block in the first
20 lines. Read it before reading the rest of the file.

- If purpose doesn't match your task; stop reading.
- Use s,e line ranges: Read(offset=s, limit=e-s) for the section you need.
- Check see before searching; the file you need may be listed.

## Before Committing Markdown Changes

1. Run: agentmap update <changed files>
2. Review output for sections marked content-changed or new.
3. Read flagged sections and update their descriptions in the nav block.
4. Commit.
<!-- /agentmap:init -->
```

Note the closing marker `<!-- /agentmap:init -->` -- this defines the block boundary for uninit to remove.

### Tool-Specific Frontmatter

Cursor (`.cursor/rules/agentmap.md`):
```yaml
---
description: How to read and maintain AGENT:NAV blocks in markdown files
globs: "**/*.md"
alwaysApply: false
---
```
`alwaysApply: false` means Cursor only loads this rule when working with .md files (via globs match) or when the model decides it's relevant.

Windsurf (`.windsurf/rules/agentmap.md`):
```yaml
---
trigger: model_decision
description: How to read and maintain AGENT:NAV blocks in markdown files
---
```
`trigger: model_decision` means Windsurf shows the description to the model; full rule loaded only if model decides it's relevant. Alternative: `trigger: glob` with `globs: ["**/*.md"]` for automatic activation on .md files.

Continue (`.continue/rules/agentmap.md`):
```yaml
---
name: agentmap
description: How to read and maintain AGENT:NAV blocks in markdown files
globs: "**/*.md"
---
```

OpenCode (`.opencode/skills/agentmap/SKILL.md`):
```yaml
---
name: agentmap-maintain
description: Read and maintain AGENT:NAV blocks in markdown files
---
```
OpenCode skills are loaded on-demand via the `skill` tool call; not always in context.

Roo Code and Amazon Q: plain markdown; no frontmatter needed.

### Go Embed

```go
package templates

import "embed"

//go:embed *.tmpl
var FS embed.FS

// Helper to read a template by name
func Get(name string) ([]byte, error) {
    return FS.ReadFile(name)
}
```

### Testing

- Each template must parse without error
- Each template must contain `<!-- agentmap:init -->` marker
- Each template must contain `<!-- /agentmap:init -->` closing marker
- No commas in template body text (would break nav block examples)
- Frontmatter in tool-specific templates must be valid YAML
close_reason: internal/templates package with Go embed; 9 templates; table-driven tests for markers/YAML/no-commas

title: 7.4 agentmap init (multi-tool + hooks)
description: Detect agent tool configs (AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md; .cursor/rules; .windsurf/rules; .continue/rules; .roo/rules; .amazonq/rules; .aider.conf.yml). Append instructions with <!-- agentmap:init --> marker. Idempotent. Pre-commit hook setup: detect pre-commit-config.yaml; husky; git hooks; lefthook (warn only). --yes/--dry-run/--tool/--all/--no-hook flags.
design:
## Multi-Tool Detection

Scan the repo root for agent tool config files. Detection order matters -- process all detected tools; not just the first match.

### Detection Table

| Check | Tool(s) Covered | Action |
|-------|----------------|--------|
| `AGENTS.md` exists | OpenCode; GitHub Copilot; Windsurf; Roo Code; Zed | Append agents.md.tmpl |
| `CLAUDE.md` exists | Claude Code | Append agents.md.tmpl |
| `.cursor/rules/` dir exists | Cursor (modern) | Write cursor.md.tmpl as `.cursor/rules/agentmap.md` |
| `.cursorrules` file exists | Cursor (legacy) | Append agents.md.tmpl |
| `.windsurf/rules/` dir exists | Windsurf | Write windsurf.md.tmpl as `.windsurf/rules/agentmap.md` |
| `.continue/rules/` dir exists | Continue.dev | Write continue.md.tmpl as `.continue/rules/agentmap.md` |
| `.roo/rules/` dir exists | Roo Code / Cline | Write roo.md.tmpl as `.roo/rules/agentmap.md` |
| `.amazonq/rules/` dir exists | Amazon Q Developer | Write amazonq.md.tmpl as `.amazonq/rules/agentmap.md` |
| `.opencode/` dir exists | OpenCode | Write opencode-skill.md.tmpl as `.opencode/skills/agentmap/SKILL.md` |
| `.aider.conf.yml` exists | Aider | WARN ONLY: print instruction to add `read: [AGENTS.md]` |
| None detected | (all cross-tool) | Create `AGENTS.md` with agents.md.tmpl (best cross-tool default) |

Important: AGENTS.md covers 5+ tools natively (OpenCode; Copilot; Windsurf; Roo Code; Zed all read it). Creating AGENTS.md is the single highest-value action.

GitHub Copilot also reads `.github/copilot-instructions.md` but AGENTS.md covers it; no need to duplicate.

Zed reads the FIRST match from: .rules; .cursorrules; .windsurfrules; .clinerules; copilot-instructions.md; AGENT.md; AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md; GEMINI.md. So if both .cursorrules and AGENTS.md exist; Zed only sees .cursorrules. This is Zed's limitation; document it but don't try to work around it.

### Idempotency

Before appending/writing; check for `<!-- agentmap:init -->` marker:
- In append targets (AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md; .cursorrules): scan file for marker string
- In create targets (.cursor/rules/agentmap.md; etc.): check if file exists and contains marker
- If marker found: skip with message `Already configured: <path>`

### Pre-Commit Hook Setup

After agent config; offer hook installation. Detection:

| Check | Action |
|-------|--------|
| `.pre-commit-config.yaml` exists | Append hook entry YAML (safe -- it's a list of repos) |
| `.husky/` dir exists | Write/append to `.husky/pre-commit` |
| `.git/hooks/pre-commit` exists | Append guard block (check for existing `agentmap check` first) |
| `.lefthook.yml` exists | WARN: "Add agentmap check to your lefthook config manually" |
| `.git/hooks/` only (no existing hook) | Offer to create `.git/hooks/pre-commit` |
| Not a git repo | WARN: "No git repo detected; skipping hook setup" |

Hook idempotency: grep for `agentmap check` in existing hook files before adding.

For .pre-commit-config.yaml: the entry uses `language: system` (not `language: python`) since agentmap is a Go binary.

### Output Format

Show a preview plan; then confirm:
```
Detected: AGENTS.md; .cursor/rules/; .pre-commit-config.yaml

Will append to: AGENTS.md (28 lines)
Will create: .cursor/rules/agentmap.md (22 lines)  
Will append to: .pre-commit-config.yaml (hook entry)
Skipped: .aider.conf.yml (manual config needed; add read: [AGENTS.md])

Proceed? [y/N]
```

Use `--yes` to skip prompt. Use `--dry-run` to only print the plan.

### Flags

- `--yes`: skip confirmation
- `--dry-run`: preview only; no writes
- `--tool <name>`: only configure a specific tool (cursor; claude; windsurf; continue; roo; amazonq; opencode; aider)
- `--all`: configure for all detected tools (this is already the default; flag exists for explicitness)
- `--no-hook`: skip pre-commit hook setup

### Safety

- NEVER overwrite existing file content -- append only (for markdown files)
- NEVER auto-edit YAML/JSON config files EXCEPT .pre-commit-config.yaml (which is a safe list append)
- For .aider.conf.yml: always warn; never modify
- Always preview before writing
- Use os.O_APPEND for file writes to markdown files
- For directory-based rules (new file creation): check existence first

title: 7.2 Install scripts (curl + PowerShell)
description: POSIX install.sh: OS/arch detection; GitHub Releases download; checksum verify; sudo escalation; --yes/--version/--bin-dir flags. PowerShell install.ps1: same for Windows; add to user PATH. shellcheck + PSScriptAnalyzer CI.
design:
## install.sh Pattern (POSIX sh)

Follow the starship.rs install script pattern -- the gold standard for curl installers. Key principles:
- Use `#!/usr/bin/env sh` (POSIX sh; NOT bash) for max compatibility
- All vars quoted; `set -eu` at top
- Colorized output with `tput` (graceful fallback to empty strings if tput missing)
- Confirmation prompt by default; `--yes` / `-y` to skip (detect piped stdin with `! [ -t 0 ]`)
- Temp dir with trap cleanup: `TMP=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP" EXIT INT TERM`

### OS/Arch Detection

```sh
detect_os() {
  os="$(uname -s)"
  case "$os" in
    Linux*)  os="Linux" ;;
    Darwin*) os="Darwin" ;;
    MINGW*|MSYS*) echo "Use install.ps1 for Windows"; exit 1 ;;
    *) echo "Unsupported OS: $os"; exit 1 ;;
  esac
}

detect_arch() {
  arch="$(uname -m)"
  case "$arch" in
    x86_64|amd64)  arch="x86_64" ;;
    aarch64|arm64) arch="arm64" ;;
    *) echo "Unsupported architecture: $arch"; exit 1 ;;
  esac
}
```

Note: archive naming from GoReleaser uses `agentmap_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz` format (title case OS; x86_64 not amd64). Match this exactly.

### Download + Verify

```sh
REPO="ryankelln/agentmap"
if [ "$VERSION" = "latest" ]; then
  URL="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/latest/download/agentmap_${os}_${arch}.tar.gz"
  CHECKSUM_URL="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/latest/download/checksums.txt"
else
  URL="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${VERSION}/agentmap_${os}_${arch}.tar.gz"
  CHECKSUM_URL="https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download/${VERSION}/checksums.txt"
fi

curl -fsSL "$URL" -o "$TMP/agentmap.tar.gz"
curl -fsSL "$CHECKSUM_URL" -o "$TMP/checksums.txt"

# Verify checksum
EXPECTED=$(grep "agentmap_${os}_${arch}.tar.gz" "$TMP/checksums.txt" | cut -d' ' -f1)
ACTUAL=$(sha256sum "$TMP/agentmap.tar.gz" | cut -d' ' -f1)
# macOS uses shasum -a 256 instead of sha256sum
if ! command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ACTUAL=$(shasum -a 256 "$TMP/agentmap.tar.gz" | cut -d' ' -f1)
fi
```

### Install Location

Default `/usr/local/bin`. Test writability before sudo:
```sh
DEST="${BIN_DIR:-/usr/local/bin}"
if [ -w "$DEST" ]; then
  mv "$TMP/agentmap" "$DEST/"
else
  echo "Need elevated permissions to install to $DEST"
  sudo mv "$TMP/agentmap" "$DEST/"
fi
```

### Flag Parsing

Parse with a while-case loop (no getopt; not portable):
```sh
while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --yes|-y) YES=1 ;;
    --version) shift; VERSION="$1" ;;
    --bin-dir) shift; BIN_DIR="$1" ;;
    *) echo "Unknown flag: $1"; exit 1 ;;
  esac
  shift
done
```

## install.ps1 Pattern (PowerShell)

```powershell
[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    [switch]$Yes,
    [string]$Version = 'latest',
    [string]$BinDir = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\agentmap"
)

$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$Arch = if ($env:PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE -eq 'ARM64') { 'arm64' } else { 'x86_64' }
$Archive = "agentmap_Windows_$Arch.zip"

# Download
$TmpDir = New-TemporaryFile | ForEach-Object { Remove-Item $_; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $_ }
try {
    # ... download zip + checksums.txt; verify; extract
    # Add to PATH if not present:
    $UserPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', 'User')
    if ($UserPath -notlike "*$BinDir*") {
        [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', "$UserPath;$BinDir", 'User')
    }
} finally {
    Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $TmpDir
}
```

Key: Windows uses .zip (not .tar.gz). `[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable` persists PATH changes across sessions.

## Testing

- Run `shellcheck install.sh` (must pass clean)
- Run PSScriptAnalyzer on install.ps1
- CI matrix: test install on ubuntu-latest + macos-latest + windows-latest
close_reason: install.sh (POSIX) and install.ps1 (PowerShell) scripts implemented; shellcheck passes; CI shellcheck job added

title: 7.1 GoReleaser + release workflow
description: .goreleaser.yaml with linux/darwin/windows x amd64/arm64; CGO_ENABLED=0; ldflags version injection; checksums.txt; Homebrew tap push. GitHub Actions release.yml on v* tags. agentmap version subcommand.
design:
## GoReleaser Config

Use GoReleaser v2 format (version: 2). Minimal config that covers the essentials:

```yaml
version: 2

builds:
  - env:
      - CGO_ENABLED=0
    goos:
      - linux
      - darwin
      - windows
    goarch:
      - amd64
      - arm64
    ldflags:
      - -s -w -trimpath
      - -X main.version={{.Version}}
      - -X main.commit={{.ShortCommit}}
    main: ./cmd/agentmap

archives:
  - name_template: >-
      {{ .ProjectName }}_
      {{- title .Os }}_
      {{- if eq .Arch "amd64" }}x86_64
      {{- else }}{{ .Arch }}{{ end }}
    format_overrides:
      - goos: windows
        formats: [zip]

checksum:
  name_template: checksums.txt

changelog:
  sort: asc
  filters:
    exclude:
      - '^docs:'
      - '^test:'
      - '^chore:'

brews:
  - repository:
      owner: ryankelln
      name: homebrew-agentmap
    homepage: https://github.com/ryankelln/agentmap
    description: Navigation maps for AI agents in markdown files
    license: MIT
```

Key details:
- `-s -w` strips debug info for smaller binaries
- `-trimpath` strips local filesystem paths (reproducible builds)
- Archive naming maps amd64 -> x86_64 for user-friendly names
- Windows gets .zip; everything else gets .tar.gz
- `brews:` (not `homebrew_casks:`) is the correct GoReleaser v2 key

## Release Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yml
name: Release
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'
permissions:
  contents: write
jobs:
  goreleaser:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: stable
      - uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7
        with:
          version: '~> v2'
          args: release --clean
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

`fetch-depth: 0` is required for GoReleaser to generate changelogs from git history.

## Version Variable

The Makefile already has VERSION and LDFLAGS. Ensure main.go has:
```go
var version = "dev"
var commit = ""
```

Add a `version` cobra subcommand that prints these. GoReleaser and Makefile both inject via ldflags.

## Validation

Run `goreleaser check` to validate the config. Run `goreleaser build --snapshot --clean` to test builds locally without releasing.
close_reason: GoReleaser v2 config; release.yml on v* tags; version+commit ldflags injection; RELEASING.md docs

title: generate: heading cluster drop and structural subtitle pruning
description:
Two improvements to nav entry selection in generate, deferred until after v0.1.0.

**1. Consecutive same-depth heading drop (always on)**
A run of consecutive headings at the same depth with no lines between them (not even blank lines) should be collapsed to the first heading only. This handles metadata blocks like '# Round: 01 / # Draft: 123' and heading clusters that waste nav budget. Implemented in generate (not the parser — parser stays faithful). See §11.3 of agentmap-design.md.

**2. Structural (lone subtitle) tagging and pruning**
A heading with no same-depth siblings under its parent is tagged structural. Structural headings are pruned first in the budget algorithm (before depth-based pruning). With --drop-subtitles flag they are excluded entirely regardless of budget. See §11.3 and §11.4 of agentmap-design.md.

Design is spec'd in agentmap-design.md. Implementation goes in generate's section-selection logic (not parser).

title: FlushState: warn when nav block unparseable but state entry present
description: When FlushState processes a file in next-state and the nav block cannot be parsed (pr.Found == false), hasRemainingAuto returns false and the entry is checked off silently. This can mask a corrupted or missing nav block — the task gets marked done even though the nav block was never actually reviewed. Should emit a warning to stderr when a state entry's file has no parseable nav block, so the user knows the entry was skipped due to a bad block rather than completed normally.

title: FindFrontmatterEnd: handle ---<content> on closing line
description: When a small model writes a nav block immediately after the YAML frontmatter closing delimiter on the same line (e.g. '---<!-- AGENT:NAV'), FindFrontmatterEnd fails to recognize the frontmatter close because TrimSpace('---<!-- AGENT:NAV') != '---'. This causes LocateNavBlock to scan from line 0 and miss the nav block entirely. Should either: (a) treat any line starting with '---' as the frontmatter close (with a warning), or (b) detect '---<!-- AGENT:NAV' as a special case and warn the user to fix it. This makes the parser more robust to small-model mistakes.
close_reason: Merged to main in feat/ws7-jz3-parser-robustness. CI green.

title: eval harness: test nav-writing-guide effectiveness across agents
description:
## Why

The nav-writing-guide.md is the primary instruction set agents use to write AGENT:NAV descriptions. We have no way to measure whether it actually works, which agents follow it well, or which parts of the instructions are failing. Without evaluation data we cannot improve the guide systematically.

## What needs to be done

Build a standalone evaluation harness (eval/ directory or scripts/eval/) that:

1. **Test fixtures** — A small corpus of markdown files with known-good ('gold') nav descriptions written by a human. These cover the key cases: purpose lines, about fields, ~ removal, > hints, see entries, anti-restatement rule. The fixtures should be realistic (not toy), sourced from real agentmap docs or similar.

2. **Task generation** — For each fixture, strip the gold descriptions back to ~ scaffolding (or auto-generated keywords), run agentmap index to produce an index-tasks.md. This is the input an agent actually receives.

3. **Agent runner** — A script or Go program that sends the index-tasks.md (with embedded nav-writing-guide) to a target LLM via its API and collects the rewritten nav block. Needs to support multiple models (e.g. Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, smaller/cheaper models). Must record token usage and cost per run.

4. **LLM judge** — A second LLM call that scores each written description against the four quality criteria from section 7 of the guide: decision support; disambiguation; precision; format compliance. The judge receives: the section content, the gold description, the agent-written description, and the rubric. Returns a score per criterion (1-3) and a brief rationale. Should be a strong model (e.g. Claude 3.5 Sonnet or GPT-4o) regardless of which model is being evaluated.

5. **Metrics and report** — Aggregate scores per model, per criterion, and per description type (purpose vs about vs see). Track: mean quality score; format violation rate (commas, >10 words, ~ not removed); cost per task; latency. Output a markdown report suitable for including in benchmarks.md or a new eval-results.md.

6. **Guide iteration workflow** — Make it easy to tweak nav-writing-guide.md and re-run the harness to see whether a change improves scores. The harness should cache agent responses (keyed by model + prompt hash) to avoid re-paying for calls when only the judge or metrics change.

## Acceptance criteria

- Can run a full eval against at least 2 models with a single command
- Produces a markdown report showing per-model scores on all four quality criteria
- Cost and token usage are reported per model
- Judge prompt is versioned alongside the guide so regressions are detectable
- Cached responses are used on re-runs when the input has not changed

## Design decisions

- Harness lives outside the main binary (no LLM calls in generate/update/check/index ever)
- Likely a standalone Go binary in cmd/agentmap-eval/ or a shell+Python script in scripts/eval/
- Judge model is configurable but defaults to a strong model; the point is scoring quality not saving money on the judge
- Gold descriptions should be written for the index-fixture/ files first (they are already realistic and tested)
- Consider OpenAI-compatible API abstraction so one runner works for Claude (via Anthropic), GPT (via OpenAI), and local models (via ollama)

title: Update generate CLI help text: --expand-threshold and --sub-threshold are pruning-only heuristics
description: The CLI help strings for --expand-threshold and --sub-threshold in cmd/agentmap/generate.go still use unconditional framing ('sections over this size get full entries'). Since agentmap-qya, these thresholds only apply when the max_nav_entries budget is exceeded. The help text should be updated to match the design doc §4.1 update from 820fe84.
close_reason: Closed

title: generate: nav inclusion should be budget-driven not depth-driven
description: The design intent is that all tracked headings get full nav entries if they fit within max_nav_entries (~20). The current implementation uses per-section sub_threshold/expand_threshold checks that are tied to h3 specifically (generate.go looks at h3 children of h2 parents). A shallow doc with 1 H1 + 2 H2 + 15 H3 = 18 headings should produce 18 full entries, not apply the three-tier threshold. The threshold heuristics should only kick in when total tracked headings would exceed the budget. Design doc updated (section 3.3, 3.8, 11.4, defaults table) to reflect budget-first approach.
close_reason: Budget-first nav pruning implemented and merged to main. Two follow-up beads created: agentmap-x2e (check.go About mutation discard), agentmap-24i (CLI help text update).

title: Refactor: shared locateNavBlock helper to eliminate insertNavBlock/findNavBlock duplication
description: insertNavBlock and findNavBlock independently implement nav-block location logic. They diverged and caused a bug (agentmap-1sp). A shared locateNavBlock(lines []string) (start, end int) helper would eliminate the duplication and prevent future drift. Discovered during agentmap-1sp fix.
close_reason: Done: LocateNavBlock unified with pure positional logic; no searchFrom/stopOnContent/MaxNavBlockStartLine. All call sites updated. commit 1927aeb.

title: Add goreleaser check to make ci target
description: Discovered during agentmap-pup: .goreleaser.yaml is only validated at release time. Running goreleaser check in make ci catches config errors early. Requires goreleaser installed in CI; can use goreleaser/goreleaser-action in a separate validate job or add to existing ci.yml.
close_reason: goreleaser-check job added to .github/workflows/ci.yml; runs on every push/PR

title: Create ryankelln/homebrew-agentmap GitHub repo
description: Prerequisite for GoReleaser brew formula push (agentmap-pup). GoReleaser will fail on first v* tag release until this repo exists. Create as empty public repo; GoReleaser writes the formula on release.
close_reason: homebrew-agentmap repo already created manually at https://github.com/RKelln/homebrew-agentmap

title: Default exclude patterns silently dropped when user overrides exclude: in agentmap.yml
description: In internal/config/config.go; Load unmarshals YAML directly over a zero-value Config; not over Defaults(). So if a user specifies exclude: [CHANGELOG.md]; the default .agentmap and .agentmap/** patterns are lost from cfg.Exclude. The SkipDir guard in discoverMD still protects against indexing .agentmap/ files; but other consumers of cfg.Exclude lose the defaults silently. Fix by merging user excludes with defaults rather than replacing.
close_reason: Fixed with tests; CI passes.

title: lineCount off-by-one via strings.Split in buildTaskEntry
description:
strings.Split(content, "
") on a file ending with 
 produces a trailing empty string; inflating lineCount by 1. Verify whether this is consistent with the rest of the codebase; if not; fix with strings.Count(content; "
") or trimming the trailing newline before splitting.
close_reason: Fixed with tests; CI passes.

title: ** glob pattern not recursive in discoverMD exclude matching
description: filepath.Match in discoverMD (internal/index/index.go) doesn't support recursive ** glob — it only matches a single path component. A user exclude pattern like docs/** will silently fail to exclude nested paths. Should document the limitation or replace with a recursive walk check.
close_reason: Fixed with tests; CI passes.

title: Document the generate-then-restore workflow for new markdown files
description: The correct workflow for adding nav blocks to a new markdown file is: (1) write the file; (2) run agentmap generate to get a nav skeleton; (3) manually write descriptions. This is undocumented and causes agents to lose hand-written nav blocks when they run generate to verify. Add a 'Starting from scratch' subsection to docs/nav-writing-guide.md or a note in AGENTS.md under 'Before Committing Markdown Changes'. Discovered during agentmap-dx5: the subagent wrote a hand-crafted nav block; ran generate; and lost it.
close_reason: Added '## 2. Starting from Scratch' section to docs/nav-writing-guide.md. Covers write-then-generate-then-rewrite workflow.

title: Add --warn-unreviewed flag to check command
description: Discovered during agentmap-gr2: check command could optionally warn about files with remaining ~ (auto-generated) descriptions. This helps agents identify files that still need description review. Flag: --warn-unreviewed. Not blocking for generate ~ prefix feature but needed for index command workflow.
close_reason: Duplicate of agentmap-vl7 which was implemented and merged

title: Add --warn-unreviewed flag to check command
description: Discovered during agentmap-gr2: check command could optionally warn about files with remaining ~ (auto-generated) descriptions. This helps agents identify files that still need description review. Flag: --warn-unreviewed. Not blocking for generate ~ prefix feature but needed for index command workflow.
close_reason: agentmap-n2u: Added integration test verifying ~ prefix preserved after update. agentmap-vl7: Added --warn-unreviewed flag to check command that warns about auto-generated descriptions.

title: Add integration test: generate → update preserves ~ prefix
description: Discovered during agentmap-gr2: update preserves ~ prefix by construction (copies About as-is) but no test verifies this end-to-end. Add a test that generates a nav block, modifies the markdown, runs update, and verifies ~ is preserved on all descriptions.
close_reason: Duplicate of agentmap-n2u which was implemented and merged

title: Add integration test: generate → update preserves ~ prefix
description: Discovered during agentmap-gr2: update preserves ~ prefix by construction (copies About as-is) but no test verifies this end-to-end. Add a test that generates a nav block, modifies the markdown, runs update, and verifies ~ is preserved on all descriptions.
close_reason: agentmap-n2u: Added integration test verifying ~ prefix preserved after update. agentmap-vl7: Added --warn-unreviewed flag to check command that warns about auto-generated descriptions.

title: Move sectionWordCount helper to navblock package
description: sectionWordCount is duplicated across internal/generate/generate.go, internal/update/update.go, and internal/check/check.go. All three compute word count for a section (content lines, heading excluded) using identical logic. Moving it to internal/navblock as navblock.SectionWordCount(lines []string, start, n int) int would eliminate the duplication and make WordCount computation the canonical responsibility of the navblock package (which already owns CountWords and NormalizeHeading). Discovered during agentmap-k5l: the duplication was accepted to avoid import cycles but navblock is importable by all three packages.
close_reason: Implemented in feat/agentmap-k5l-polish alongside agentmap-k5l

title: Fix TestRepoChanges_TempRepo to avoid os.Chdir (use dir param in RepoChanges)
description: TestRepoChanges_TempRepo uses os.Chdir to point RepoChanges() at a temp directory. This is fragile in parallel test runs. RepoChanges() should accept an optional directory parameter (or an internal helper should), allowing the test to pass the temp dir directly. Discovered during agentmap-k5l review: S5.
close_reason: Implemented in feat/agentmap-k5l-polish alongside agentmap-k5l

title: Extract normalizeHeading helper shared by generate, update, check
description: generate.go, update.go, and check.go all independently apply the same comma-stripping + hash-stripping logic to heading names when building nav entry keys. A single normalizeHeading(text string) string helper (or heading.Normalize) would centralize the rule. Discovered during agentmap-k5l review: C1 (comma key mismatch in check) was a direct consequence of this duplication.
close_reason: Implemented in feat/agentmap-k5l-polish alongside agentmap-k5l

title: Refactor ParseNavBlock to return ParseResult struct instead of 5-value tuple
description: ParseNavBlock currently returns (NavBlock, int, int, bool, bool) — 5 values. This is hard to read and extend. Replace with a ParseResult{Block, Start, End, Found, Corrupted} struct. Discovered during agentmap-k5l: the 5-value return was introduced as a quick extension of the existing signature, but it's a code smell that will compound as the function evolves.
close_reason: Implemented in feat/agentmap-k5l-polish alongside agentmap-k5l

title: Add round-trip test for nav block generate/parse/render
description: Discovered during agentmap-atl: no integration test verifies that generate → parse → render produces identical output. Add a round-trip test that generates a nav block, parses it back, renders it again, and confirms the output matches.
close_reason: Added round-trip tests: render→parse→render→compare. Table-driven tests for full block, purpose-only, empty about. Merged in feat/agentmap-ea3-cna-nav-validation-roundtrip

title: Add round-trip test for nav block generate/parse/render
description: Discovered during agentmap-atl: no integration test verifies that generate → parse → render produces identical output. Add a round-trip test that generates a nav block, parses it back, renders it again, and confirms the output matches.
close_reason: Added N>0 validation with stderr warnings and round-trip tests for nav block render/parse/render

title: Add nav block validation for N > 0
description: Discovered during agentmap-atl: parseNavEntry accepts N=0 or negative N without validation. This produces confusing error messages in check and update. Add validation that N > 0 and emit a clear error or warning when parsing corrupted nav blocks.
close_reason: Added N>0 validation with stderr warnings in parseNavEntry. Merged in feat/agentmap-ea3-cna-nav-validation-roundtrip

title: Add nav block validation for N > 0
description: Discovered during agentmap-atl: parseNavEntry accepts N=0 or negative N without validation. This produces confusing error messages in check and update. Add validation that N > 0 and emit a clear error or warning when parsing corrupted nav blocks.
close_reason: Added N>0 validation with stderr warnings and round-trip tests for nav block render/parse/render

title: 7.9 GitHub Action for agentmap check
description: Composite action (action.yml): install agentmap from GitHub Releases; run agentmap check; annotate failures with ::error workflow commands. Inputs: version (default latest); path (default .). Usage: 2 lines of YAML in consumer CI. Stretch: PR comment on failure.
design:
## Composite GitHub Action

Host as `action.yml` in a dedicated `ryankelln/agentmap-action` repo (or at the root of the agentmap repo if keeping it simple for v0.1).

### action.yml

```yaml
name: 'agentmap check'
description: 'Validate AGENT:NAV blocks are in sync with markdown headings'
branding:
  icon: 'map'
  color: 'blue'
inputs:
  version:
    description: 'agentmap version to install (e.g. v0.1.0 or latest)'
    required: false
    default: 'latest'
  path:
    description: 'Path to check (default: current directory)'
    required: false
    default: '.'
runs:
  using: 'composite'
  steps:
    - name: Install agentmap
      shell: bash
      run: |
        curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ryankelln/agentmap/main/install.sh \
          | sh -s -- --yes --version ${{ inputs.version }}
    - name: Check nav blocks
      shell: bash
      run: agentmap check ${{ inputs.path }}
```

### Consumer Usage

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
jobs:
  agentmap:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ryankelln/agentmap-action@v1
```

Or with pinned version:
```yaml
      - uses: ryankelln/agentmap-action@v1
        with:
          version: v0.1.0
          path: docs/
```

### Error Annotations

The `agentmap check` output format already includes file paths and details. To get GitHub PR annotations; the check command should output lines in the format:
```
::error file=docs/auth.md::AGENT:NAV out of sync: ##Token Exchange nav says 14-35 actual 16-37
```

This means `check` needs a `--format github` flag (or auto-detect `GITHUB_ACTIONS=true` env var) that outputs workflow command annotations instead of plain text. This is a small addition to the check command in Phase 4.

### Stretch: PR Comment on Failure

Use `gh` or the GitHub API via `actions/github-script` to post a comment with the check report and fix instructions. This requires `pull-requests: write` permission. Defer to v0.2.

### Testing

- Test action in the agentmap repo's own CI (self-check)
- Create a test fixture repo with valid and invalid nav blocks
- Verify exit code 0 on valid; exit code 1 on invalid
- Verify the install script works in GitHub Actions Ubuntu runner (which already has curl)
close_reason: Closed

title: 7.7 Package manager support
description: Homebrew tap via GoReleaser (ryankelln/homebrew-agentmap repo). Scoop bucket via GoReleaser scoops: section (ryankelln/scoop-agentmap repo). go install already works. Document all methods in README.
design:
## Package Managers

### Homebrew Tap (automated by GoReleaser)

GoReleaser's `brews:` section auto-pushes a formula to `ryankelln/homebrew-agentmap` on every release. Pre-requisite: create the `ryankelln/homebrew-agentmap` GitHub repo (empty; GoReleaser writes the formula).

The GITHUB_TOKEN in the release workflow needs write access to the tap repo. Either:
- Use a PAT with `repo` scope (set as `HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` secret)
- Or give the default GITHUB_TOKEN access if both repos are in the same org

Users install with:
```bash
brew install ryankelln/tap/agentmap
```

### Scoop Bucket (automated by GoReleaser)

Add to .goreleaser.yaml:
```yaml
scoops:
  - repository:
      owner: ryankelln
      name: scoop-agentmap
    homepage: https://github.com/ryankelln/agentmap
    description: Navigation maps for AI agents in markdown files
    license: MIT
```

Pre-requisite: create `ryankelln/scoop-agentmap` GitHub repo.

Users install with:
```powershell
scoop bucket add agentmap https://github.com/ryankelln/scoop-agentmap
scoop install agentmap
```

### go install (already works)

No changes needed. `go install github.com/ryankelln/agentmap/cmd/agentmap@latest` works because the module is properly structured with go.mod.

### Documentation

All methods documented in README Install section (see 7.6 bead for exact content).
close_reason: Closed

title: 7.6 README agent section
description: Add 'For AI Agents' section to README.md: what agentmap does; agentmap init as setup command; link to AGENTS.md. Update Install section with curl; Homebrew; PowerShell; go install methods.
design:
## README Agent Section

Add a section near the top of README.md (after 'Why'; before 'Commands') that speaks directly to agents.

### Content

```markdown
## For AI Agents

If you are an AI coding agent: `agentmap` adds navigation maps to markdown
files so you can read exactly the section you need instead of entire files.

To set up agentmap in your user's project:

\`\`\`bash
agentmap init
\`\`\`

This detects your agent tool (Claude Code; Cursor; Windsurf; Continue; Roo Code;
OpenCode; GitHub Copilot; etc.) and installs the right configuration files.

See [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for the full workflow: how to read nav blocks;
how to update them after editing markdown files.
```

### Install Section Update

Replace the current Install section with all methods:

```markdown
## Install

\`\`\`bash
# macOS / Linux
curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ryankelln/agentmap/main/install.sh | sh

# Windows (PowerShell)
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ryankelln/agentmap/main/install.ps1 | iex

# Homebrew
brew install ryankelln/tap/agentmap

# Go
go install github.com/ryankelln/agentmap/cmd/agentmap@latest

# Upgrade
agentmap upgrade
\`\`\`
```

Keep 'build from source' as a secondary option below.

### Placement

The README heading order should be:
1. What It Does (existing)
2. Why (existing)
3. For AI Agents (new)
4. Install (updated)
5. Quick Start (existing)
6. Commands (existing)
7. Configuration (existing)
8. Design (existing)
9. Contributing (existing)
10. License (existing)
close_reason: Closed

title: refine benchmark inputs for realistic keyword mix
description: Discovered during agentmap-epi: current benchmarks use highly repetitive synthetic text, which is good for stability but weak at representing realistic keyword extraction behavior. Add a more varied fixture set and, if useful, smaller microbenchmarks around parser/navblock hot spots.
close_reason: Closed

title: perf: benchmark generate command performance
description: Discovered during agentmap-b2f: design spec requires update/check under 100ms for 50 files but no benchmark tests exist. Add benchmarks for generate and keyword extraction.
close_reason: Benchmarks already exist: BenchmarkFileDryRun in generate_test.go, BenchmarkExtractKeywords and BenchmarkExtractPurpose in keywords_test.go

title: Add CLI smoke test for command registration
description: Discovered during agentmap-8yx: cmd/agentmap/main.go has no test file. Add at least a smoke test verifying command registration and flag parsing.
close_reason: CLI smoke tests added: 8 tests covering root help, version, flag parsing, and stub output. Switched commands to cmd.Println for testability.

title: polish: pre-commit hook, performance, error handling
description:
Add pre-commit hook template, ensure update/check under 100ms for 50 files, consistent error wrapping (fmt.Errorf context: %w), --llm flag stub. See tasks.md Phase 6.

Edge cases (from agentmap-dae):
- Nav block corruption handling: warn to stderr if parser cannot parse existing nav block, treat as no block, offer regenerate
- Large file cap: if nav block would produce >20 entries, only include h1 and h2 regardless of subheading threshold
- Empty sections: heading immediately followed by another heading (s == e), include in nav with empty about
- Special chars in headings: strip commas, strip leading/trailing whitespace
close_reason: All items implemented and merged to main: hook subcommand, batch git diff, error wrapping, nav corruption, comma stripping, soft nav cap with WordCount, ParseResult struct, NormalizeHeading, SectionWordCount

title: config and file discovery
description: Add agentmap.yml config loader (min_lines, sub_threshold, expand_threshold, max_depth, exclude) with defaults. Implement file discovery via git ls-files (respects .gitignore) with fallback to filepath.Walk. Handle edge cases: nav block corruption, empty sections, special chars in headings, large file cap. See tasks.md Phase 5.
close_reason: Config loader and file discovery implemented in agentmap-8yx. Remaining edge cases (corruption handling, large file cap) tracked separately.

title: Consolidate insertNavBlock duplication between generate and update
description: Both internal/generate/generate.go and internal/update/update.go have near-identical insertNavBlock and cleanBlankLines functions. This duplication caused update.go to miss the dirty frontmatter close fix initially (agentmap-jz3). Collapse these into a single shared helper in the navblock package to prevent future inconsistencies.

title: Extract shared IsFrontmatterCloseLine helper
description: Both navblock.FindFrontmatterEnd and parser.isFrontmatterClose duplicate the same frontmatter close detection logic. Extract a shared helper like navblock.IsFrontmatterCloseLine(line string) (isClose, dirty bool) to eliminate duplication and prevent drift on future changes.

title: check.go discards About mutations from PruneNavEntries
description: check.go calls PruneNavEntries for entry-count validation but discards the About (hint) mutations on parent entries. Not a bug today since check never writes files. However, if check ever gains a --fix mode, this silent discard will cause hints to be lost. A comment was added in 820fe84 noting the issue. This bead tracks fixing it if/when --fix is added to check.

title: Refactor: unify maxNavBlockStartLine constant across navblock and generate packages
description: maxExistingBlockLine=20 is declared independently in insertNavBlock (generate.go), ParseNavBlock (navblock.go), and ParseFilesBlock (navblock.go). A single exported navblock.MaxNavBlockStartLine constant would make the policy a single source of truth. Discovered during agentmap-1sp review.
close_reason: Done: LocateNavBlock unified with pure positional logic; no searchFrom/stopOnContent/MaxNavBlockStartLine. All call sites updated. commit 1927aeb.

title: Deduplicate shared template body into a single source
description: Discovered during agentmap-3vh: all 7 .md.tmpl files share identical body text. If agent workflow instructions change; all 7 files need updating in sync. Consider a build-time generator or a _body.tmpl read by a codegen step to reduce drift risk.
close_reason: Closed

title: RenderFilesBlock produces malformed output for unsorted input
description: RenderFilesBlock (internal/navblock/navblock.go) emits directory prefix lines based on the current entry's dir vs the previous entry's dir. If entries are not sorted by directory; the same directory prefix may be emitted multiple times; producing a block that ParseFilesBlock reconstructs incorrectly. Either sort inside RenderFilesBlock or add a doc comment stating the caller must sort.
close_reason: Closed

title: --force flag is a silent no-op when combined with --files-only
description: In indexCmd; when --files-only is set; force is parsed but never used. Users who pass --force --files-only get no warning. Either document the combination as unsupported; or emit a warning when force && filesOnly.
close_reason: Closed

title: IndexInlineMax boundary (exactly N entries) untested
description: WriteFilesBlock uses len(entries) <= cfg.IndexInlineMax. The exact boundary (entries == IndexInlineMax) is not covered by tests. Add a table-driven test case for the boundary value.
close_reason: Closed

title: agentmap check should print a success message when all blocks are in sync
description: agentmap check currently exits 0 with no output on success. This makes it hard to distinguish 'check passed' from 'no files found'. Should print 'All nav blocks in sync (N files)' or similar on success. Discovered during agentmap-dx5: check returned empty log which was confusing.
close_reason: Implemented: check prints 'All nav blocks in sync (N files checked)' on exit 0 via cmd.OutOrStdout(). Singular form for 1 file. Tests added.

title: Fix 20 vs 40 line inconsistency in agentmap-design.md
description: agentmap-design.md section 3.5 says 'read the first 40 lines' but section 7.2 example says 'reads first 20 lines'. AGENTS.md and the nav-writing-guide both say 40. The design doc needs to be updated to use 40 consistently. Discovered during agentmap-dx5 review.
close_reason: Fixed: updated all 'first 20 lines' references to 'first 40 lines' in agentmap-design.md; added rationale explaining the 40-line budget in §3.5

title: cleanup: remove dead extractPurpose function
description: Discovered during agentmap-b2f: extractPurpose() in internal/generate/generate.go is no longer called since purpose generation moved to keywords.ExtractPurpose. Remove the dead code.
close_reason: extractPurpose already removed from codebase — no dead code remains
